title: ERGOTHIONEINE
description: Ergothioneine is a naturally occurring sulfur-containing amino acid and potent antioxidant that is primarily obtained through dietary sources like mushrooms and absorbed via the highly specific OCTN1 (ETT) transporter. It acts as a cytoprotective agent by accumulating in tissues subject to high oxidative stress, where it helps protect DNA, proteins, and lipids from damage while supporting mitochondrial function.

research.title: Ergothioneine promotes longevity and healthy aging in male mice
research.summary: Long-term oral supplementation in mice significantly extended lifespan and reduced age-related declines in movement velocity, cognitive function, and hippocampal neurogenesis.

research.title: Ergothioneine: A Stress Vitamin with Antiaging, Vascular, and Neuroprotective Roles?
research.summary: This review analyzes ergothioneine's role as a potential 'longevity vitamin' that protects against oxidative stress-induced damage in vascular and neural systems.

research.title: Ergothioneine for cognitive health, longevity and healthy ageing: where are we now?
research.summary: A comprehensive review of the current evidence supporting ergothioneine's ability to support brain health and mitigate cognitive decline in aging populations.

research.title: Ergothioneine as a functional nutraceutical: Mechanisms, bioavailability, and therapeutic implications
research.summary: Researchers explore the compound's potent anti-inflammatory and cytoprotective properties, highlighting its unique transport mechanism into human cells.

sentiment.summary: Users overwhelmingly view Ergothioneine as a 'foundational' longevity supplement with high interest in its neuroprotective benefits and lack of acute side effects.

effects.label: Cumulative Benefits:
effects.description: Users note that effects are subtle and build over months rather than providing an immediate 'rush.'

effects.label: Cognitive Maintenance:
effects.description: Highly effective in animal models for rescuing memory deficits and promoting hippocampal neurogenesis.

effects.label: Biomarker Improvement:
effects.description: Research indicates significant suppression of markers for kidney stress and metabolic decline.

effects.label: Transport Specificity:
effects.description: Exceptionally bioavailable due to the body's dedicated OCTN1 transporter, ensuring high cellular absorption.

dosage.label: Standard Maintenance:
dosage.description: 5mg to 10mg daily is the most common dosage for general healthy aging.

dosage.label: Therapeutic Range:
dosage.description: Some users and studies explore up to 25mg-30mg per day for targeted cognitive support.

dosage.label: Body Weight Scaling:
dosage.description: Clinical studies in mice utilize 4-5 mg/kg, which translates to moderate human doses to achieve longevity effects.

dosage.label: Mushrooms as Source:
dosage.description: Dietary intake of 100g of mushrooms periodically is suggested as a natural alternative to purified supplements.

sideEffects.label: Minimal Reported:
sideEffects.description: Most users report zero acute side effects even with daily long-term administration.

sideEffects.label: Digestive Comfort:
sideEffects.description: Unlike some antioxidants, it is generally well-tolerated by the stomach.

sideEffects.label: Unknown Long-term in Humans:
sideEffects.description: While mouse data is positive, some users express caution regarding the lack of multi-decade human clinical trials.

availability.label: Increasing Market:
availability.description: Widely available as a fermented or synthetic ingredient (e.g., ErgoActive) in high-end longevity brands.

availability.label: Dietary Sources:
availability.description: Highest concentrations are found in specialty mushrooms like Porcini, Oyster, and Shiitake.
